title: [InstCombine] Relax the conditons of fold of `ucmp`/`scmp` into phi by allowing the phi node to use the result of `ucmp`/`scmp` more than once

tests.source_program:
define i1 @different_blocks_multiple_phi_uses_no_multi_edge(i1 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  br i1 %c, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  br label %merge

bb2:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %phi = phi i8 [ %cmp, %bb1 ], [ %cmp, %bb2 ]
  %r = icmp slt i8 %phi, 0
  ret i1 %r
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @different_blocks_multiple_phi_uses_no_multi_edge(i1 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  br i1 %c, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  %cmp1 = icmp slt i16 %x, %y
  br label %merge

bb2:
  %cmp2 = icmp slt i16 %x, %y
  br label %merge

merge:
  %phi = phi i1 [ %cmp1, %bb1 ], [ %cmp2, %bb2 ]
  ret i1 %phi
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: This test has multiple phi uses of the cmp result from DIFFERENT blocks (bb1 and bb2), not multi-edges from the same block. The summary focuses on duplicate destinations from switch creating multi-edges from one block, but this benefits from hasOneUser relaxation without any multi-edge deduplication needed.

alive2.src:
define i1 @different_blocks_multiple_phi_uses_no_multi_edge(i1 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  br i1 %c, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  br label %merge

bb2:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %phi = phi i8 [ %cmp, %bb1 ], [ %cmp, %bb2 ]
  %r = icmp slt i8 %phi, 0
  ret i1 %r
}

source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i1 @different_blocks_multiple_phi_uses_no_multi_edge(i1 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:
  br i1 %c, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:                                              ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

bb2:                                              ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %bb2, %bb1
  %r = icmp slt i16 %x, %y
  ret i1 %r
}

define i1 @different_blocks_multiple_phi_uses_no_multi_edge(i1 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = scmp i8 i16 %x, %y
  %#range_0_%cmp = !range i8 %cmp, i8 255, i8 2
  br i1 %c, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb2:
  br label %merge

bb1:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %phi = phi i8 [ %#range_0_%cmp, %bb1 ], [ %#range_0_%cmp, %bb2 ]
  %r = icmp slt i8 %phi, 0
  ret i1 %r
}

bb2:
  br label %merge

bb1:

merge:

}

tests.source_program:
define i1 @ucmp_variant_unsigned_comparison(i8 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = call i8 @llvm.ucmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  switch i8 %c, label %bb_2 [
    i8 0, label %bb
    i8 1, label %bb
  ]

bb_2:
  br label %bb

bb:
  %phi = phi i8 [ %cmp, %entry ], [ %cmp, %entry ], [ 0, %bb_2 ]
  %r = icmp ult i8 %phi, 0
  ret i1 %r
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @ucmp_variant_unsigned_comparison(i8 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = call i8 @llvm.ucmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  switch i8 %c, label %bb_2 [
    i8 0, label %bb
    i8 1, label %bb
  ]

bb_2:
  br label %bb

bb:
  %cmp.bb = icmp ult i16 %x, %y
  %r = phi i1 [ %cmp.bb, %entry ], [ %cmp.bb, %entry ], [ 0, %bb_2 ]
  ret i1 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The existing test and summary focus on scmp (signed compare), but this uses ucmp (unsigned compare). While the patch handles both, the summary doesn't explicitly cover unsigned comparison intrinsics or the ult icmp pattern that results.

bb:

define i1 @three_plus_edges_same_block_dedup(i8 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  switch i8 %c, label %bb_2 [
    i8 0, label %bb
    i8 1, label %bb
    i8 2, label %bb
  ]

bb_2:
  br label %bb

bb:
  %phi = phi i8 [ %cmp, %entry ], [ %cmp, %entry ], [ %cmp, %entry ], [ 0, %bb_2 ]
  %r = icmp slt i8 %phi, 0
  ret i1 %r
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @three_plus_edges_same_block_dedup(i8 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  switch i8 %c, label %bb_2 [
    i8 0, label %bb
    i8 1, label %bb
    i8 2, label %bb
  ]

bb_2:
  br label %bb

bb:
  %cmp.bb = icmp slt i16 %x, %y
  %r = phi i1 [ %cmp.bb, %entry ], [ %cmp.bb, %entry ], [ %cmp.bb, %entry ], [ 0, %bb_2 ]
  ret i1 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The summary mentions 'multiple incoming edges' but the known test only shows 2 edges from the same block. This test has 3+ edges from entry to bb, testing the deduplication logic more thoroughly than the documented pattern.
